meta.title: archy, cracy

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: archy, cracy
root.mentor_note: archy and cracy both mean rule; which one a word uses is mostly a matter of history, not meaning.

attempt.direction_prompt: Shared among representatives, or held by one ruler?

options.text: In the hands of one ruler

context_sentences: The country had abolished its monarchy a century ago, though the old palace still drew tourists.
Under the monarchy, no law took effect until it carried the queen’s own signature.
The country’s young democracy held its first genuinely contested election only two years after the war.
Critics argued that a healthy democracy needs a free press at least as much as it needs free elections.
Within days of the government’s collapse, the capital had slid into open anarchy.
The novel imagines a city surviving years of anarchy purely on the strength of informal, unwritten rules.

construct_options.text: A state with no government, law, or order
A government ruled by a single all powerful leader
A government in which power is shared by all citizens equally

context_sentences: Investigators described the country’s economy as an oligarchy, controlled by fewer than a dozen families.
What had begun as a democracy had quietly hardened into an oligarchy over three decades.

construct_options.text: Government in which power rests with a small group of powerful people
Government in which power is held by a single religious leader
A state with no government or ruling structure at all
